level: Bohr-Fermi (g_e = 2 exactly, no anomalous moment, no recoil, no nuclear structure, no multi-loop QED)

outputs_with_reduced_mass_for_context.note: Reduced-mass factor (1 + m_e/m_p)^{-3} is a recoil correction and is NOT part of strict Bohr-Fermi at g_e=2. Reported here for context: it explains roughly half of the strict residual.

fermi_contact_prefactor_(8 pi / 3).source: Standard Pauli (Dirac NR limit) Fermi contact term. Comes from <delta(r)> matrix element of the spin-spin magnetic interaction. Not GeoVac-specific.

g_e_equals_2.source: Tree-level Dirac equation. The Camporesi-Higuchi Dirac operator on S^3 (geovac.dirac_s3) gives the same g_e = 2 at tree level via |lambda_n| = n + 3/2, but this is the Dirac equation acting (g_e = 2 is forced by the gamma matrix algebra), not a GeoVac-specific result.

mu_B_equals_alpha_over_2.source: Bohr magneton in atomic units: e h_bar / (2 m_e c) = alpha/2 with c = 1/alpha. Standard atomic-unit identity, uses CODATA alpha as input.

mu_N_equals_(m_e/m_p)_alpha_over_2.source: Nuclear magneton in atomic units: scales mu_B by m_e/m_p. Uses CODATA m_e/m_p as external input.

g_p_factor.source: Proton g-factor — a measured property of the proton's internal QCD structure. NOT computable from GeoVac at this stage (would require lattice QCD or the full SM with strong-coupling content). External CODATA input. The Fermi-contact Hamiltonian carries the full g_p, not g_p/2 — the latter equals mu_p/mu_N and only appears if one writes mu_p = (g_p/2) mu_N as an expectation value rather than the operator g_p mu_N S.

psi_1s_squared_at_origin.source: Symbolic evaluation of geovac.dirac_matrix_elements._hydrogenic_radial_wavefunction(n=1, l=0, r, Z) at r=0, multiplied by |Y_{0,0}|^2 = 1/(4 pi). GeoVac uses the *continuum* hydrogenic radial wavefunction as the spatial profile of the Fock-projected n-shell. The graph spectrum n^2 - 1 is exact; the spatial profile psi_1s(r) is the framework's continuum embedding (Section 12, algebraic registry; Section 4, dimensionless vacuum principle). Honest flag: |psi(0)|^2 is a continuum quantity, not a graph quantity. The framework does not yet have a *graph-native* (non-continuum) notion of |psi(0)|^2.

alpha_squared_overall_factor.source: Two factors of alpha/2 from the magneton pair, giving alpha^2/4. The other factors absorb into the (4/3) in the canonical form. External calibration via alpha.

reduced_mass_factor_(NOT_INCLUDED_IN_STR.source: Recoil correction; corresponds to the proton not being infinitely heavy. Briefing says 'no recoil', so this factor is excluded from the headline number but reported for context. Standard physics (reduced-mass coordinate transformation in two-body bound state).

honest_assessment.psi_1s_squared_at_origin_(Z3/pi): A. From GeoVac: geovac/dirac_matrix_elements.py::_hydrogenic_radial_wavefunction evaluated symbolically at r=0. Spherical Y_{0,0} normalization (1/(4 pi)) is standard. Note: this is a continuum quantity (the wavefunction profile in the framework's continuum embedding), NOT a graph-native quantity — see CLAUDE.md Section 4.
honest_assessment.g_e = 2: B. Standard physics: tree-level Dirac equation. GeoVac's Camporesi-Higuchi Dirac operator on S^3 has eigenvalues |lambda_n| = n + 3/2 and g_e = 2 falls out of the same gamma-matrix algebra. Not GeoVac-specific.
honest_assessment.Fermi contact prefactor (8 pi / 3): B. Standard physics: comes from the delta-function spin-spin Hamiltonian of QED in the non-relativistic limit (Bethe-Salpeter Eq. 22.18). Could in principle be re-derived from a graph-native Fermi contact integral on S^3 (an open question for future GeoVac work), but at present we use the standard prefactor.
honest_assessment.mu_B = alpha/2 in atomic units: B. Standard physics: atomic unit identity using c = 1/alpha. External calibration enters via alpha (CODATA).
honest_assessment.mu_N = (m_e/m_p) alpha/2: C + B. Standard physics; m_e/m_p is external CODATA input.
honest_assessment.g_p (proton g-factor): C. External calibration (CODATA). NOT computable in current GeoVac — requires QCD/lattice physics. Treat as input.
honest_assessment.alpha (fine-structure constant): C. External calibration (CODATA). Paper 2 in Observations is the framework's open conjecture about deriving alpha from the Fock-projected S^3 spectral data; we do NOT use that here. alpha is supplied as CODATA input.
honest_assessment.1 Hartree = 6.580e15 Hz: C. Unit conversion only, used to render the result in MHz.
honest_assessment.Reduced-mass factor (1 + m_e/m_p)^{-3}: Excluded from headline result per briefing. Standard physics two-body recoil; would require a recoil-aware Fock projection to be derivable from GeoVac (currently not available).

interpretation.expected_dominant_correction: a_e ~ alpha / (2 pi) ~ 1161 ppm (electron anomalous moment, Schwinger 1948). Track HF-2 will derive this from the GeoVac vertex correction machinery.
interpretation.secondary_correction: (1 + m_e/m_p)^{-3} ~ -1635 ppm (reduced-mass / recoil). The strict Bohr-Fermi over-shoots experiment because no recoil and no electron anomalous moment cancel each other partially; including recoil but not a_e undershoots, including a_e but not recoil overshoots.
